recording_notes: MONITOR RECORDING — DIFFERENT SETUP. RCA monitor on yellow basket/crate, directly facing camera. Different lab station — 'No Food or Drink' sign visible at left. DIFFERENT FOAM (brighter red vs pink). 1920x1080 at 30fps. Moderate debris on board. DIFFERENT SUTURE COLOR — appears pink/purple rather than dark/black (may be RCA color rendering or genuinely different suture material). Best direct-facing monitor framing in series but on a smaller/different monitor.

frame_analyses.description: Drain centered on bright red foam. Different setup — monitor on yellow basket, 'No Food/Drink' sign visible. Moderate debris. No instruments.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: DIFFERENT STATION/SETUP from V25-V27. Equipment change may affect performance.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments at drain. One metallic from left, one from upper right. Suture visible. Active needle driving.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instruments entered ~8-10s. Placement in progress at 30s — normal pace so far.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments at drain with suture visible extending downward. Pink/purple suture clearly visible. First throw in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement complete ~35s. First throw at 60s — 25s into throw, slower than usual.

frame_analyses.description: ENORMOUS suture arcs extending across entire field. Instrument from lower left. Suture loops spanning the full width of the view. Tightening a throw with massive excess suture.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: At 2.5 minutes, still in throws. Suture management severely degraded. Excess suture length causing tangles.

frame_analyses.description: Large pink/purple suture loops visible across field. Both instruments at drain. Multiple suture crossings. Extremely tangled suture management.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 3 minutes in, still throwing. Suture tangles are consuming enormous time. Possible re-wraps needed.

frame_analyses.description: Instrument from upper right at drain. Multiple suture crossings visible over drain. Knot area complex with dark and purple lines.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 4 minutes. Transitioning to cutting or final throw tightening. Extremely prolonged throw sequence.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments at drain. Knot visible. Dark suture and purple suture visible. Cutting in progress. Drain shifted position.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 5 minutes. Cutting phase but extremely slow. Tangled suture making cutting difficult.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments crossed in X at drain. Suture tails extending laterally. Still cutting at 5.5 minutes. Knot visible at center.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Still cutting. The tangled suture requires multiple cuts and careful work.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments at drain. Blue instrument from left, metallic from upper right. Long suture tails still visible. Tangled loops above drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 6 minutes. Still cutting. This is the longest cutting phase in the entire series.

frame_analyses.description: Task complete. Instruments withdrawn. Knot visible at drain center with short tails. Drain slit appears closed. Drain not avulsed despite the prolonged manipulation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completion ~375s. Total task time from first instrument: ~365s. WORST PERFORMANCE IN ENTIRE SERIES.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw extremely prolonged (~55s). Suture management issues evident. Multiple attempts likely.
Second throw ~70s. Enormous suture arcs visible — excess suture causing tangles.
Third throw ~80s. Most prolonged. Suture tangles severely impaired efficiency.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Placement appeared normal (~23s). The catastrophic regression started AFTER placement.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Despite the extreme time, final result at 378s shows drain closed with compact knot and short tails. Drain not avulsed — remarkable given 6 minutes of manipulation. Quality of final product appears acceptable despite the time.

technique_summary: V28 at approximately 365 seconds is the WORST PERFORMANCE IN THE ENTIRE 28-VIDEO SERIES — 85% slower than V26's 94s record set potentially in the same session, and 67% slower than even the V20 crash (218s). The catastrophic regression appears driven by THREE COMPOUNDING FACTORS: (1) DIFFERENT EQUIPMENT/STATION — new monitor setup, different foam, possibly different instruments; (2) DIFFERENT SUTURE — appears pink/purple rather than the familiar dark/black, suggesting a different material with different handling properties; (3) FATIGUE — if this follows V26-V27 in the same session. The placement phase (23s) was normal, indicating the core needle driving skill transferred. But every throw was catastrophically slow (55s, 70s, 80s — totaling ~205s vs typical 50-67s) and cutting consumed 135s. The visible suture tangles suggest the different suture material (if confirmed) was the primary disruptor — unfamiliar handling properties made every wrapping motion take 3-4x longer. The FINAL PRODUCT paradoxically looks acceptable — short tails, closed drain, no avulsion — suggesting the trainee persevered through difficulty to complete correctly.
improvement_suggestions: CRITICAL: Identify what changed. Was this a DIFFERENT SUTURE MATERIAL? The pink/purple color suggests possibly Vicryl or a different brand vs the standard 2-0 silk.
If different suture: this is expected. Suture handling skills are partially suture-specific. Practice with the new material will bring times down rapidly.
If same suture, different color rendering: the equipment change alone (different station, monitor, possibly different instruments) can cause significant regression.
The 365s time should NOT be compared to V26's 94s in the same performance trajectory — this is effectively a different task with different equipment.
POSITIVE: Placement was normal (23s). The core skill transferred. Only suture manipulation was affected.
POSITIVE: The trainee completed the task correctly despite 6+ minutes of difficulty. Perseverance and quality maintained.
If this equipment/suture will be used again, do 3-5 dedicated practice runs to build familiarity before recording.
Consider this V28 as a new 'baseline' for this specific equipment configuration, not a regression in the V1-V27 arc.
strengths: COMPLETED the task despite extreme difficulty — perseverance
Final product quality appears acceptable — short tails, closed drain, no avulsion
Placement at 23s — core needle driving skill transferred to new equipment
Drain not avulsed despite 6 minutes of manipulation — 28 consecutive videos
Better monitor framing — direct camera view of RCA monitor

phase_comparison.placement_23s: NORMAL. V27: 18s. V26: 16s. Core skill intact.
phase_comparison.throws_205s: CATASTROPHIC. V27: 67s. V26: 50s. 3-4x slower. Suture tangles.

key_observations: 365s / 227 FLS — WORST IN ENTIRE SERIES. Worse than baseline (198s) and V20 crash (218s).
DIFFERENT EQUIPMENT: New station, new monitor setup, different foam, possibly different suture material.
Pink/purple suture visible — different from standard dark/black. If different material, this explains everything.
Placement (23s) was NORMAL — only post-placement phases were catastrophically affected.
Throws consumed 205s (normally 50-67s) — 3-4x slower. Visible suture tangles in every frame.
Cutting consumed 135s (normally 26-67s) — tangled suture made every cut difficult.
DESPITE the catastrophe: task completed correctly, drain closed, no avulsion, short tails.
This should be classified as an EQUIPMENT CHANGE event, not a skill regression.
If same session as V26-V27: extreme fatigue + equipment change = perfect storm.
28th video. The training arc remains valid for V1-V27. V28 is an outlier caused by external factors.

series_statistics.note: V28 should be flagged as equipment-change outlier, not included in skill trajectory analysis
